name: Agent-First Config
synopsis: Generic dot-path accessor for JSON/TOML/YAML/dotenv/INI configs, usable both as a Rust library and as a standalone afconfig CLI binary. The library is the primary interface: spores embed it for generic dot-path access without writing a dispatch table per field. The CLI wraps the same logic for shell scripts and quick one-off reads or edits from any language.
intent: Provide a reusable config traversal and mutation library that works with any serde struct
Enable dot-path get/set operations (e.g., imap.host, identities.me.email) without enumeration in Clap
Support slug-based array access (keyed lists) alongside nested object and scalar paths
Keep the lib lightweight with independently gated JSON, TOML, YAML, dotenv, and INI backends
Publish the agent-first-config skill so agents use afconfig for safe reads and mutations
Validate arbitrary serde config values through a fallible typed adapter
Preserve unrelated source bytes during supported JSON, TOML, YAML, dotenv, and INI edits
Solve the consistency problem in multi-format tools where config CLI patterns diverge
mutations: Rework all format mutations to be source-preserving — set/unset/add/remove edit only the addressed span and leave comments, ordering, whitespace, quoting, anchors, and number/datetime spelling byte-identical, or fail before writing.
Add first-class dotenv and INI Core v1 backends with documented, side-effect-free dialects (string leaves, no variable expansion, no process-environment access).
Make each config format an independent Cargo feature (json/toml/yaml/dotenv/ini), all enabled by default.
Add four mutually-exclusive secret write sources (--value-secret, --value-secret-stdin, --value-secret-prompt, --value-secret-fd) that keep the value out of argv, logs, success payloads, and errors.
Add a global --secret-name to redact exact field names in non-targeted output while a targeted get still returns the requested value.
Add get --value-only to emit a bare scalar for shell command-substitution under a strict raw-output and exit-status contract.
Route the whole CLI through Agent-First Data: structured results and errors, --output json|yaml|plain, conventional -V, and predictable exit codes on every path.

Add a typed serde adapter (from_value/to_value) that validates after mutation with path-aware errors, plus Value::Unsigned for lossless u64 with explicit rejection when a format cannot represent a number.

Make keyed-list add/remove fully generic via an explicit --slug-field (fixing nested dotted prefixes) and adopt one unambiguous dot-path grammar with backslash escaping for literal dots and backslashes.

Write files atomically (temp + fsync + rename, preserving mode) and refuse to mutate symlink and hardlink targets; publish an agent-first-config skill and remove the placeholder default/doc/schema commands.
